title: STAT+: With successful trials, Roche takes its MS drug to regulators, but safety questions loom
summary: The Swiss drugmaker Roche presented the latest data for its experimental multiple sclerosis drug, setting the stage for the company to seek approval.

title: STAT+: From Revolution Medicines, more strong data on KRAS drug and a glimpse of a ‘novel class’ beyond it
summary: A company that is changing the outlook on pancreatic cancer has more promising data for treatments near-term and for a generation beyond.

title: STAT+: Kyverna Therapeutics plans to submit cell therapy for stiff person syndrome for FDA approval
summary: A one-time cell therapy from Kyverna Therapeutics for stiff person syndrome, a rare disease, improved mobility and reduced disabilities in a late-stage trial.

title: STAT+: At AACR, more strong results for Revolution Medicine’s KRAS drug, plus assurance from NCI’s director
summary: At AACR, Revolution Medicines presented more eye-catching data about its KRAS-targeting treatment, and Anthony Letai declared the state of cancer research steady.

title: STAT+: Key GOP senators push back on Trump’s plan to cut NIH, reorganize HHS
summary: Lawmakers seem poised to reject cuts to biomedical research and public health spending for a second straight year.

title: STAT+: Insurers refuse to join Medicare pilot offering weight loss drugs to seniors at steep discount
summary: Health insurers would have likely faced a financial strain by participating in the Medicare pilot.

title: Tortugas debuts with $106M and brain drugs from Hansoh, Eisai
summary: Led by former Sage Therapeutics executives Jeff Jonas and Al Robichaud, the company is starting out with a portfolio of neurological medicines in clinical testing.

title: Merck’s fast-ascending kidney cancer drug hits a setback
summary: Welireg’s failure in first-line kidney cancer removes a blockbuster opportunity and could leave an opening for a rival drug from Arcus Biosciences, analysts said.

title: Flu vaccine no longer mandated for U.S. troops, defense secretary says
summary: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth announced Tuesday that the U.S. military will no longer require all American troops to get the flu vaccine.

title: STAT+: UnitedHealth earnings beat expectations in the first quarter, but hurdles persist
summary: UnitedHealth’s financial performance in the first quarter far exceeded analyst’s expectations in both its Optum and UnitedHealthcare divisions.

title: STAT+: RFK Jr. to face four GOP doctors at Senate hearings
summary: We'll be zeroed in on Kennedy's exchanges with one doc in particular: Bill Cassidy.

title: BioAge says early data suggest ‘best-in-class’ potential for inflammation drug
summary: The company has rallied from an earlier setback in obesity thanks to early progress for a drug targeting “NLRP3,” an inflammasome linked to a variety of health conditions.

title: Opinion: STAT+: I’m an expert on presidential health. The 25th Amendment is not an option for removing Trump
summary: Those calling for Trump’s removal under the 25th Amendment are misguided. Here’s why, writes Lawrence K. Altman.

title: Judge postpones OxyContin-maker Purdue Pharma’s sentencing to let opioid victims attend in person
summary: A judge is expected to sentence OxyContin maker Purdue Pharma to forfeit $225 million to the Justice Department on Tuesday, clearing the way for the company to finalize a settlement…

title: STAT+: CMS to ask every state for new audits of health care providers
summary: The Trump administration has been heavily focused on combating purported fraud in federal health care programs.

summary: BioAge bets on inflammation, a short Pfizer career, and more biotech news from The Readout

title: This CDC conference was always open to the public. Until now

title: STAT+: Kennedy hints at coming breakthrough device announcement
summary: In this edition of STAT Health Tech: Health secretary hints at possible announcement on how breakthrough medical devices are paid for, and more.

title: STAT+: BioAge says experimental pill aimed at reducing heart risks significantly reduced inflammation
summary: BioAge Labs said its investigational pill for cardiovascular risk prevention significantly reduced inflammation in an early study.

title: STAT+: Tortugas Neuroscience launches with hopes to develop drugs for brain disorders, other conditions
summary: Tortugas Neuroscience launched Tuesday with plans to develop drugs licensed from Chinese drugmaker Jiangsu Hansoh and Japanese pharmaceutical Eisai.

title: Serif, Flagship’s latest biotech, aims to make a new kind of genetic medicine
summary: The startup is developing “modified DNA” therapies it claims can combine the strengths of multiple approaches, from messenger RNA to gene therapy.

summary: When a CEO develops acquired executive dysfunction yet remains in their role, the challenges are significant.

summary: A reconstruction layer has fallen on medicine. It asks: Was the original decision justified?

title: Trump executive order lifts psychedelics biotechs
summary: Meant to speed research and access to psychedelics for mental health conditions, the order is the latest in a series of developments that, to Wall Street, make psychedelics a more “investable space.”

title: RFK Jr. defends HHS tenure, 12% proposed budget cut
summary: Republicans largely praised the HHS secretary’s track record during a marathon of House hearings last week, but some raised concerns about proposed budget cuts to the National Institutes of Health.

title: Nektar surges again as alopecia drug shows new promise in extension study
summary: Patients who'd seen substantial hair regrowth in the first part of Nektar’s trial had a "deepening" treatment response, extending a comeback bid that’s caused shares to skyrocket over the last year.

title: Lilly boosts ‘in vivo’ cell therapy capabilities with Kelonia buyout
summary: Worth up to $7 billion, Lilly’s second recent acquisition of a company in the space is centered around a cell therapy that’s currently in early-stage testing for multiple myeloma.

title: FDA Achieves Year 1 Goals in Reducing Animal Testing in Drug Development

summary: Featured at this year’s meeting on early cancer research were results from an immunotherapy Merck acquired in 2024 and several targeted medicines for lung tumors.

summary: This podcast explores how clinical trial supplies move from early planning to global delivery, ensuring investigational medicines reach patients safely and on time.
